steps.instruction: Write out the factors in the numerator and denominator.
Identify the highest common factor (HCF) of the numerator and denominator.
steps.working: The HCF of 10 and 4 is 2, and $x$ appears in both. So the HCF is $2x$.

steps.instruction: Divide both numerator and denominator by the HCF.

title: Adding algebraic fractions with different denominators

steps.instruction: Find the lowest common denominator (LCD) of 4 and 6.
steps.working: Multiples of 4: 4, 8, 12, 16, $\ldots$ Multiples of 6: 6, 12, 18, $\ldots$ The LCD is 12.

steps.instruction: Convert each fraction to an equivalent fraction with denominator 12.
Add the numerators, keeping the denominator the same.
Rewrite the division as multiplication by the reciprocal of the second fraction.
Multiply numerators together and denominators together.
Simplify by cancelling common numeric and variable factors.
